{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Development Representative",
  "description": "Egatee is the No. 1 B2B E-Commerce, a high-tech platform company in Africa. The head office is in Hong Kong, China. We are operating well in Uganda, Ghana, Cote d&#39;Ivoire, and India. The mission of the company is to empower retail customers to easily manage a large number of commodities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Development Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;| On-site

Role Overview


	We are seeking a proactive Sales Development Representative to drive top-of-funnel growth for our consumer goods brands in Lagos.
	You&rsquo;ll generate and qualify leads, initiate conversations with retailers/distributors/buyers, and book sales meetings for our Account Managers.
	This role is ideal for someone who knows how to sell fast-moving consumer products and can open doors quickly.


Key Responsibilities


	Prospect and contact potential B2B/B2C buyers via calls, WhatsApp, email, and store visits
	Generate qualified leads for fabrics, beauty/skincare, home appliances, and kitchenware product lines
	Pitch product benefits, pricing, and promotions to distributors, wholesalers, supermarkets, salons, and online vendors
	Book product demos/sampling sessions and hand off qualified opportunities to closers
	Hit monthly KPIs for outbound touches, qualified leads, and meetings booked
	Maintain accurate pipeline records in CRM/spreadsheet and report weekly on activity
	Gather market feedback on pricing, competitor moves, and customer objections.


Ideal Candidate


	HND / BSc preferred but not required if experience is solid
	1 - 3 years in field sales, B2B sales, retail distribution, or telesales for FMCG/consumer products
	Must have proven experience selling at least two of the following: Fabrics/textiles, Beauty/Skincare, Home Appliances, Kitchenware
	Strong network with Lagos-based traders, stores, salons, markets like Balogun, Yaba, Ikeja, Trade Fair
	Excellent persuasion and negotiation skills; comfortable with daily targets and rejection
	Smart, presentable, and able to move around Lagos for client visits
	Basic WhatsApp/Excel skills for reporting.


Salary
N300,000 &ndash; N500,000 monthly + Commission &amp; Performance Bonuses.

Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;christopher.aimiton@egatee.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-23",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Egatee Online Ecommerce"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "197890"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Sales Development Representative Job at Egatee Online Ecommerce




        
    

        
        Egatee is the No. 1 B2B E-Commerce, a high-tech platform company in Africa. The head office is in Hong Kong, China. We are operating well in Uganda, Ghana, Cote d'Ivoire, and India. The mission of the company is to empower retail customers to easily manage a large number of commodities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Development Representative

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time | On-site

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive Sales Development Representative to drive top-of-funnel growth for our consumer goods brands in Lagos.
	You’ll generate and qualify leads, initiate conversations with retailers/distributors/buyers, and book sales meetings for our Account Managers.
	This role is ideal for someone who knows how to sell fast-moving consumer products and can open doors quickly.


Key Responsibilities


	Prospect and contact potential B2B/B2C buyers via calls, WhatsApp, email, and store visits
	Generate qualified leads for fabrics, beauty/skincare, home appliances, and kitchenware product lines
	Pitch product benefits, pricing, and promotions to distributors, wholesalers, supermarkets, salons, and online vendors
	Book product demos/sampling sessions and hand off qualified opportunities to closers
	Hit monthly KPIs for outbound touches, qualified leads, and meetings booked
	Maintain accurate pipeline records in CRM/spreadsheet and report weekly on activity
	Gather market feedback on pricing, competitor moves, and customer objections.


Ideal Candidate


	HND / BSc preferred but not required if experience is solid
	1 - 3 years in field sales, B2B sales, retail distribution, or telesales for FMCG/consumer products
	Must have proven experience selling at least two of the following: Fabrics/textiles, Beauty/Skincare, Home Appliances, Kitchenware
	Strong network with Lagos-based traders, stores, salons, markets like Balogun, Yaba, Ikeja, Trade Fair
	Excellent persuasion and negotiation skills; comfortable with daily targets and rejection
	Smart, presentable, and able to move around Lagos for client visits
	Basic WhatsApp/Excel skills for reporting.


Salary
N300,000 – N500,000 monthly + Commission & Performance Bonuses.

Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Lead",
  "description": "Beneprojecti Nigeria Limited is an Oil and Gas EPCMM (Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Management and Marine) Company. We have a reliable team of professionals and qualified engineers with good experience in projects and contract management capabilities to provide quality service to Marine, Energy, Oil and Gas sector of the Nigerian economy. However, we have partnership/ alliances with other reputable servicing companies for the purpose of executing specific projects both Onshore &amp; Offshore in line with the local content policy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Increase the business services of the company in the IOCs.
	Prepare bids and submit on time as requested.
	Establish and maintain work relationships with clients and regulatory bodies E.g. NAPIMS, NCDMB, NUIMS and DPR.


Educational Qualifications


	Must possess at least a Second Class Degree in Engineering or any Science-related courses.
	Must have a minimum of 4 years of working experience in a similar position with prior experience in Marine, Energy, Oil and Gas in the Onshore and Offshore industry.
	Must have extensive working experience in the oil and gas sector i.e Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Marine, Management (EPCMM).
	Must have an eye for details and Zero tolerance for rework or errors.
	Must be able to work under pressure and able to challenge deadlines.
	Must show proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint.
	Must have proposal management experience.
	Must demonstrate previous exposure to contracts and an understanding of basic contract language.
	Provide proven leadership and management experience.
	Have extensive sales experience.
	Possess experience interacting with executive-level staff.
	Must Show an understanding of the bid process.
	Experience in securing large contracts.
	Knowledge of document management methods.


Method of Application

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@beneprojecti.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Business Development Lead Job at Beneprojecti Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Beneprojecti Nigeria Limited is an Oil and Gas EPCMM (Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Management and Marine) Company. We have a reliable team of professionals and qualified engineers with good experience in projects and contract management capabilities to provide quality service to Marine, Energy, Oil and Gas sector of the Nigerian economy. However, we have partnership/ alliances with other reputable servicing companies for the purpose of executing specific projects both Onshore & Offshore in line with the local content policy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Description


	Increase the business services of the company in the IOCs.
	Prepare bids and submit on time as requested.
	Establish and maintain work relationships with clients and regulatory bodies E.g. NAPIMS, NCDMB, NUIMS and DPR.


Educational Qualifications


	Must possess at least a Second Class Degree in Engineering or any Science-related courses.
	Must have a minimum of 4 years of working experience in a similar position with prior experience in Marine, Energy, Oil and Gas in the Onshore and Offshore industry.
	Must have extensive working experience in the oil and gas sector i.e Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Marine, Management (EPCMM).
	Must have an eye for details and Zero tolerance for rework or errors.
	Must be able to work under pressure and able to challenge deadlines.
	Must show proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint.
	Must have proposal management experience.
	Must demonstrate previous exposure to contracts and an understanding of basic contract language.
	Provide proven leadership and management experience.
	Have extensive sales experience.
	Possess experience interacting with executive-level staff.
	Must Show an understanding of the bid process.
	Experience in securing large contracts.
	Knowledge of document management methods.


Method of Application

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Marketer Job at Egatee Online Ecommerce




        
    

        
        Egatee is the No. 1 B2B E-Commerce, a high-tech platform company in Africa. The head office is in Hong Kong, China. We are operating well in Uganda, Ghana, Cote d'Ivoire, and India. The mission of the company is to empower retail customers to easily manage a large number of commodities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketer

Job Locations: Lagos Island & Trade Fair, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are hiring proactive Marketers to drive sales of diverse consumer products including clothing, jewelry, cosmetics, and other fast-moving items across our Lagos Island and Trade Fair locations.


Key Responsibilities


	Actively promote and sell a wide range of products: clothing, jewelry, cosmetics, accessories, etc.
	Engage walk-in customers, explain product features/benefits, and close sales to meet daily/weekly targets
	Provide excellent customer service, handle inquiries, and build repeat customer relationships
	Maintain product displays and ensure stock is properly arranged and accounted for
	Track sales, give daily reports, and provide market feedback on customer preferences
	Achieve individual and team sales targets set by management


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE/OND.
	1+ years of experience in sales, marketing, or retail; experience with fashion/beauty products is a plus
	Strong communication and persuasive skills with good spoken English
	Neat appearance, customer-friendly, and target-driven attitude
	Must be resident in or able to commute easily to Lagos Island or Trade Fair.


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Front Office Manager",
  "description": "Expected to open 2025, Hyatt Regency Lagos Ikeja is ideally located in the serene GRA and just moments away from Murtala Muhammed International Airport. Hyatt Regency Lagos Ikeja is a premier hotel in Lagos, Nigeria, offering the perfect blend of convenience and elegance where our guests feel comfortable, connected, and encouraged to be their best selves because they are welcome and relaxed in our care.

Hyatt Regency hotels and resorts are founded on openness - our colleagues consistently serve with open minds and open hearts to deliver unforgettable celebrations, effortless relaxation and notable culinary experiences alongside expert meetings and technology-enabled collaboration.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Front Office Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About The Role


	The Assistant Front Office Manager supports the Front Office Manager in overseeing the daily operations of the front desk, ensuring exceptional guest service, efficient operations, and adherence to Hyatt brand standards.
	This role assists in managing staff, handling guest concerns, and optimizing revenue through effective room management.


Key Responsibilities
Operations Management:


	Assist in supervising daily front office operations including reception, concierge, and guest services.
	Ensure smooth check-in and check-out processes.
	Monitor room availability, reservations, and room allocations.
	Ensure compliance with Hyatt policies and procedures.


Guest Experience:


	Maintain high standards of guest service in line with Hyatt&rsquo;s service culture.
	Handle VIP guests, special requests, and guest complaints professionally.
	Ensure prompt resolution of guest issues to enhance satisfaction.


Team Supervision:


	Supervise, train, and mentor front office staff.
	Assist in staff scheduling and performance management.
	Conduct briefings and ensure team alignment with service standards.


Financial &amp; Administrative Duties:


	Assist in managing departmental budgets and controlling costs.
	Monitor daily revenue reports and occupancy levels.
	Support upselling strategies to maximize room revenue.


Coordination &amp; Communication:


	Coordinate with housekeeping, reservations, and maintenance departments.
	Ensure accurate communication of guest needs across departments.
	Maintain proper documentation and reporting.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in hospitality management, Business Administration, or related field.
	3 - 5 years of experience in front office operations, preferably within a luxury hotel brand.
	Strong knowledge of hotel management systems (OPERA).
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong leadership and problem-solving abilities.
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-24",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Assistant Front Office Manager Job at Hyatt Regency




        
    

        
        Expected to open 2025, Hyatt Regency Lagos Ikeja is ideally located in the serene GRA and just moments away from Murtala Muhammed International Airport. Hyatt Regency Lagos Ikeja is a premier hotel in Lagos, Nigeria, offering the perfect blend of convenience and elegance where our guests feel comfortable, connected, and encouraged to be their best selves because they are welcome and relaxed in our care.

Hyatt Regency hotels and resorts are founded on openness - our colleagues consistently serve with open minds and open hearts to deliver unforgettable celebrations, effortless relaxation and notable culinary experiences alongside expert meetings and technology-enabled collaboration.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Assistant Front Office Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About The Role


	The Assistant Front Office Manager supports the Front Office Manager in overseeing the daily operations of the front desk, ensuring exceptional guest service, efficient operations, and adherence to Hyatt brand standards.
	This role assists in managing staff, handling guest concerns, and optimizing revenue through effective room management.


Key Responsibilities
Operations Management:


	Assist in supervising daily front office operations including reception, concierge, and guest services.
	Ensure smooth check-in and check-out processes.
	Monitor room availability, reservations, and room allocations.
	Ensure compliance with Hyatt policies and procedures.


Guest Experience:


	Maintain high standards of guest service in line with Hyatt’s service culture.
	Handle VIP guests, special requests, and guest complaints professionally.
	Ensure prompt resolution of guest issues to enhance satisfaction.


Team Supervision:


	Supervise, train, and mentor front office staff.
	Assist in staff scheduling and performance management.
	Conduct briefings and ensure team alignment with service standards.


Financial & Administrative Duties:


	Assist in managing departmental budgets and controlling costs.
	Monitor daily revenue reports and occupancy levels.
	Support upselling strategies to maximize room revenue.


Coordination & Communication:


	Coordinate with housekeeping, reservations, and maintenance departments.
	Ensure accurate communication of guest needs across departments.
	Maintain proper documentation and reporting.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in hospitality management, Business Administration, or related field.
	3 - 5 years of experience in front office operations, preferably within a luxury hotel brand.
	Strong knowledge of hotel management systems (OPERA).
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong leadership and problem-solving abilities.
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR, Admin & Accounts Officer",
  "description": "Cyforce Security ?ff?r? ??u w?th th? r?ght ???ur?t? ??lut??n? th?t m??t ??ur n??d? ?nd budg?t. W?th a w??lth ?f ?x??r??n?? ?n ?r?v?d?ng g??d ???ur?t? ???t?m?, w? ?n?ur? th?t ??ur ??r??nn?l ?r? ??f? fr?m h?rm ?nd ???r?t??n? ??n run ?? ?m??thl? ?nd ?ff????ntl? ?? ?????bl? w?th?ut h?ndr?n??.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR, Admin &amp; Accounts Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Adamawa

Core Responsibilities


	HR &amp; Guard Welfare: Oversee the full recruitment lifecycle and staff leave schedules. You are responsible for monitoring digital attendance and ensuring the overall wellbeing and morale of our security personnel.
	Discipline &amp; SOPs: Act as the primary enforcer of office discipline and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to maintain our elite service standards.
	Accounts &amp; Money Management: Manage the branch Imprest Account for daily operational expenses. You are responsible for accurate bookkeeping, expense tracking, and transparent fund management.
	Payroll &amp; Salary Management: Maintain the branch Nominal Roll and execute accurate, timely monthly Salary Payments for all personnel.
	Branch Compliance &amp; Documentation: You will manage and verify essential operational forms to ensure high performance:
	
		Client Feedback Forms: Actively collecting and analyzing data to ensure 100% client satisfaction.
		Weekly Rotation Forms: Verifying guard deployments for accuracy.
		Supervision Forms: Tracking patrol logs and guard performance in the field.
		Monthly NSCDC Reports: Preparing and ensuring timely submission.
		Note: Statutory filings (ITF, NSITF, etc.) are handled by the Head Office.
	
	
	Customer Service &amp; Portal Management: Act as the primary contact for inquiries. All official billing, invoicing, and documentation must be conducted via the Cyforce Security Office Portal: cyforcesecurity.com/office.
	Marketing: Drive brand awareness and manage social media updates to promote branch activities and recruitment.


Requirements &amp; Stipulations


	Qualifications: Candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 1 to 3 years of experience.

	Termination Policy: Either party may terminate employment with two (2) weeks&#39; written notice.
	Age: 21 to 45


Work Schedule &amp; Remuneration


	Working Days: Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays + the last three (3) working days of every month.
	Remuneration: Salary is Negotiable (Commensurate with experience and commitment to excellence).
	Data Allowance: Provided monthly to facilitate constant portal access.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR, Admin & Accounts Officer

Job Location: Adamawa

Core Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	HR & Guard Welfare: Oversee the full recruitment lifecycle and staff leave schedules. You are responsible for monitoring digital attendance and ensuring the overall wellbeing and morale of our security personnel.
	Discipline & SOPs: Act as the primary enforcer of office discipline and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to maintain our elite service standards.
	Accounts & Money Management: Manage the branch Imprest Account for daily operational expenses. You are responsible for accurate bookkeeping, expense tracking, and transparent fund management.
	Payroll & Salary Management: Maintain the branch Nominal Roll and execute accurate, timely monthly Salary Payments for all personnel.
	Branch Compliance & Documentation: You will manage and verify essential operational forms to ensure high performance:
	
		Client Feedback Forms: Actively collecting and analyzing data to ensure 100% client satisfaction.
		Weekly Rotation Forms: Verifying guard deployments for accuracy.
		Supervision Forms: Tracking patrol logs and guard performance in the field.
		Monthly NSCDC Reports: Preparing and ensuring timely submission.
		Note: Statutory filings (ITF, NSITF, etc.) are handled by the Head Office.
	
	
	Customer Service & Portal Management: Act as the primary contact for inquiries. All official billing, invoicing, and documentation must be conducted via the Cyforce Security Office Portal: cyforcesecurity.com/office.
	Marketing: Drive brand awareness and manage social media updates to promote branch activities and recruitment.


Requirements & Stipulations


	Qualifications: Candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 1 to 3 years of experience.
	Background Verification: Candidates must consent to continuous background checks (criminal records, past employment, and character references).
	Commitment: Strict adherence to the Cyforce Security Code of Conduct is mandatory.
	Termination Policy: Either party may terminate employment with two (2) weeks' written notice.
	Age: 21 to 45


Work Schedule & Remuneration


	Working Days: Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays + the last three (3) working days of every month.
	Remuneration: Salary is Negotiable (Commensurate with experience and commitment to excellence).
	Data Allowance: Provided monthly to facilitate constant portal access.
